title: hieracium cochlearioides subsp. pseudoglanduliferum
intro: Hieracium cochlearioides subsp. pseudoglanduliferum, also known as Hieracium pseudoglanduliferum, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion genus, characterized by its toothed leaves and yellow flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use as a diuretic and its ability to treat kidney stones and other urinary tract issues. The health benefits of Hieracium cochlearioides subsp. pseudoglanduliferum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, as well as tinctures and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
